rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL MURRAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike X-Complaints
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 28, 2013, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT JOSEPH CASSIDY, CENTRIX BUILDERS, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Strike Cross-Complaints Denied. The motion is untimely. (CCP § 425.16(f).) Cross-Defendants Joseph Cassidy and Centrix Builders, Inc. ("Cross-Defendants") have not provided any basis for the court to exercise its discretion to consider the motions on the merits. Cross-Defendants have answered the Cross-Complaints. (On July 30, 2012, Mr. Flood served his Cross-Complaint, and Cross-Defendants filed their answer on August 21, 2012. On September 28, 2012, 973 Market Associates, LLC, Mr. O'Donoghue, and Mr. Manning, Mr. Murphy and Ms. Quigley separately served by mail their First Amended Cross-Complaints, and Cross-Defendants filed their answer on November 9, 2012. The First Amended Cross-Complaints filed on September 28, 2012 did not substantively change the allegations against Cross-Defendants. (Opposition, Leon Decl., 13.) On October 4, 2012, Mr. Walsh served by mail his Cross-Complaint, and Cross-Defendants filed their answer on November 9, 2012.) Cross-Defendants filed the instant motion on January 29, 2013. Cross-Defendants did not request permission to extend the time to file this motion or provide a sufficient explanation for their delay in filing this motion in their moving papers or reply. Neither the demurrers by other cross-defendants or the appointment of a referee on January 14, 2013 provides an adequate basis to extend the time under CCP § 425.16(f) for Cross-Defendants to file this motion. Request for attorney fees denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GELAREH RAHBAR DDS VS. JENNIFER BATOON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Post Judgment Atty Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 28, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT JENNIFER BATOON'S Ntc Of Motion For Post Judgment Atty Fees Granted, as set forth herein. Defendant is entitled to attorney fees for the appeal. The billing rates for the appeal are reasonable. Defendant is awarded $51,997 in attorney fees, and $621.10 in costs, for a total amount of $52,618.10 for attorney fees and costs in connection with the appeal. The Defendants billing rates for routine collection work is excessive. For this work, defendant may recover $150 hour. There were approximately 28 hours of routine collection work, for a total of $4200. For the motion to amend the judgment and for preparation of this motion for fees, Defendants' proffered billing rates are reasonable. For the motion to amend judgment, Defendant sought $23,888. This appears excessive, including excessive hearing time, and three attorneys working on the reply papers. The Court awards $20,000. For the fee motion, Defendant sought $20,322.50. This appears excessive for the preparation of an additional fee motion in the same case. The Court awards $12,500. The Court awards $2,681.45 in costs/expenses. Grand total amount of fees and costs is $91,999.95. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MUGISHA M VS. ST CHARLES BORROMEO SCHOOL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Pltf'S Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Req.'S For Documents; Req. For Admissions To Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 28, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF MUGISHA M.'s motion To Compel Answers To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Documents; Request For Admissions To Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted - no opposition filed. Court will grant the requested relief and sanctions in the amount of $900. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Establishing The Sequence Of Discovery And For An Order Enforcing The Court'S Jun-21-12 Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 28, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 3, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI's (Amended) Motion For A Protective Order Establishing The Sequence Of Discovery And For An Order Enforcing The Court'S Jun-21-12 Order. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court has reviewed the moving and opposing papers, the Register of Actions, the Court's ruling of Feb. 5, 2013, and the history pertaining to discovery in this case. Due to severely limited judicial resources, the volume of documents to be reviewed, the multiple discovery orders already in place, and that the present motion is only one in a continuum of discovery motions and disputes, the Court will appoint a discovery referee sua sponte pursuant to C.C.P. section 639(a)(5) [See Weil and Brown, et al., CAL. PRAC. GUIDE: CIV. PRO BEFORE TRIAL (The Rutter Group 2012), §§ 8:1804, et seq]. Pursuant to C.C.P. section 640(b), the parties shall meet and confer on an agreed discovery referee or, failing agreement, shall submit to the Court at the time of hearing on the motion up to three nominees for appointment as referee. The Court will also consider appointment of either Richard Best or Everett Hewlett as the referee, and the parties may so stipulate to that appointment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
